Download CHELSA climate data

chelsa.Rd

This function builds URLs and downloads CHELSA climate data based on variables, years, months, climate models, and scenarios. Uses the helper function `download_file` to handle downloads. Each raster layer is downloaded, processed (cropped/masked/resampled), added to the output stack immediately, and temporary files are deleted to minimize memory use.

Usage

chelsa(
  x,
  vars,
  years = NULL,
  months = NULL,
  gcm = NULL,
  rcp = NULL,
  ssp = NULL,
  cruts_years = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

x

A SpatRaster or sf object to define the area of interest

vars

A character vector of variables to download (e.g., "tas", "pr", "bio")

years

A character or numeric vector of years or year ranges (e.g., "1981-2010", 2015)

months

A numeric vector (1–12) specifying which months to download. If NULL and `years` are single years, all 12 months are downloaded.

gcm

General Circulation Model(s) for future projections

rcp

Representative Concentration Pathway for CMIP5 data

ssp

Shared Socioeconomic Pathway for CMIP6 data

cruts_years

Numeric vector. Years to download from CHELSAcruts (must be 1901–2016)

...

Additional arguments (currently unused)

Value

A SpatRaster stack or data.frame with extracted values

On this page

Developed by Andrea Simoncini, Michele Bertoncini, Andrea Cerofolini, Andrea Dalpasso, Mattia Falaschi, Gentile Francesco Ficetola, Elia Lo Parrino.
